extracted_knowledge.title: Longitudinal association between eye gaze fixation on social information and neurodevelopmental symptoms in children
extracted_knowledge.abstract: AIM: This large-scale, longitudinal, community-based study...
key_findings: Shorter eye gaze fixation on social info at age 4 predicted ADHD hyperactivity/inattention at ages 6-7 (n=2,101)
Also predicted emotional problems and peer problems (ASD-related)
First large-scale longitudinal demonstration of eye tracking predicting later ADHD symptoms
extracted_knowledge.methods_detail: Community-based cohort (n=2,101); eye tracking at age 4; SDQ at ages 6-7; GLM analysis
limitations: Single eye-tracking metric (fixation duration on social info)
SDQ is screening tool, not diagnostic interview

knowledge_gaps: Only one eye-tracking metric used — multi-metric approach needed
